name: Kamala's husband (Doug)
subject: Sending you some photos from Kamala’s campaign launch:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
You already know how proud I am of my amazing wife, Kamala, but today, “proud” is an understatement. This afternoon, with more than 20,000 supporters in her hometown of Oakland, Kamala officially kicked off our campaign for president. She fired up the crowd with her vision for a campaign OF the people and FOR the people. Earlier in the week, I sent you some behind-the-scenes photos from “Day 1” -- the day Kamala announced she’s running. So many supporters wrote nice notes back, so I wanted to send you some more photos from today’s HUGE campaign kick-off rally. Check them out: _What an amazing day!_ _Kamala, me, Kamala's sister Maya, and Maya's husband Tony._ _We don't have an official count yet, but early reports say that more than 20,000 supporters joined us today in Oakland._ Today was incredible, and not just because of Kamala (though, as her husband, I’m extremely biased). **Today was incredible because of the people -- each and every person who took time out of their Sunday, in person or online, to participate in this historic moment.** I’m grateful for all of you who joined us today and excited to meet those of you we’ll see along the long road to 2020. We’re in it to win it. All my best, Doug Emhoff P.S. You know I have to ask: Kamala’s not taking a dime from corporate PACs in her campaign for president: she’s relying on grassroots donations from supporters like you. **Can you pitch in a donation of $10 or more today to help power her historic campaign?** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $10 now](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-190127-oakland?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=em190127-full&amount=10&express_lane=true&t=1&akid=2184%2E5755789%2EHNj6eq)

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: We must continue fighting for reproductive rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Good news is so hard to come by in today's politics, so I wanted to make sure you saw this headline. _**"Judge Blocks Trump 's Attempt to Roll Back Birth Control Mandate**
A federal judge on Sunday granted a request by more than a dozen states to temporarily block the Trump administration from putting into effect new rules that would make it easier for employers to deny women health insurance coverage for contraceptives."_
\-- _The New York Times_ ; 1/14/2019 I'm encouraged the courts have temporarily stopped this administration from making it easier for employers to deny birth control. No employer should come between a woman and her doctor. It's a victory -- **but we must remember it is also temporary.** Trump and Republicans will continue to come after women's reproductive rights by any means necessary -- including using the courts. We must continue to fight their relentless assault. [**Add your name and show your support for access to reproductive care >>**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cda/622b7b5b/1e9af958/307b7532/3691339999/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e190128m") Thank you. Debbie
---

subject: Thanks to President Trump, China's Economy Is Rapidly Decelerating
body:
President Trump is exposing China’s economic vulnerability. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9807e8a463&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Thanks to President Trump, China's Economy Is Rapidly Decelerating**

“President Trump is exposing China’s economic vulnerability, showing that the communist nation cannot maintain its rapid growth without it patently unfair mercantilist trade policies,” former executive Andy Puzder writes in Fox News.

“For the first time in decades, the U.S. has openly challenged Beijing’s aggressive trade manipulations, slapping targeted counter-tariffs on Chinese goods and pressuring the Communist regime to change its protectionist policies. So far, the strategy has been remarkably successful, forcing Beijing to lower its tariffs on U.S. automobile manufacturers by 25 percent while continuing to negotiate with the White House on a potential trade deal.”

[Click here to continue reading.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=58818d9093&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| Lost amid the mainstream media’s coverage of the border security debate is this factoid from a recent ABC/_Washington Post_ poll: “Most American voters believe the United States is doing ‘too little’ to keep illegal aliens from coming to the United States,” Timothy Meads reports for _[Townhall](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0b57e46f46&e=3ee4985eea)_. That figure includes four in five Republicans, more than a third of Democrats, and a majority of independents, Meads adds.
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration’s announcement Wednesday to recognize Venezuelan National Assembly leader Juan Guaidó as interim president of Venezuela amid the current popular uprising against the anti-democratic socialist Maduro regime was an act of brilliant statecraft,” Jon Hartley writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=56934afd78&e=3ee4985eea). “Thanks to the U.S.-led coalition endorsing Juan Guaidó as interim president, Venezuelans now have hope for a new leader who has promised to host free and fair elections.”
---
|
---
| Yesterday marked the observance of International Holocaust Remembrance Day. In _[Forbes](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b555d29929&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Ewelina U. Ochab discusses the Elie Wiesel Act that President Trump signed earlier this month—“an act aimed at improving the US response to mass atrocities.” Among other measures, the act prioritizes the prevention of genocide as a matter of America’s national security interest.

**More** : [Read President Trump’s Proclamation for Holocaust Remembrance Day](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=710354b3e2&e=3ee4985eea)
---

Peter,

Rumors are swirling that a challenger could announce against us as soon as February or March.

name: Kamala Harris
subject: What we’re fighting for:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAMALA HARRIS FOR THE PEOPLE
body:
|
#
Yesterday, in front of more than 20,000 people in my hometown of Oakland, we officially kicked off this campaign for president. It was just a couple blocks from yesterday’s rally that, nearly 30 years ago, as a young district attorney, I walked into the courtroom for the very first time and said five words that would guide my life’s work: **“Kamala Harris, for The People.”** For The People isn’t just our campaign slogan: it’s the heart and soul of our movement, the very reason I wake up every morning ready to fight so hard to win this race. I promise to stand up and fight for YOU. If you’re with me in this, one of the most important ways you can show your support is by making an early contribution to our campaign. Help us end January strong and show the world what this grassroots team is capable of. **Will you pitch in a contribution of $10 to our campaign? We’ve taken on many battles together. We’ve won and we’ve lost. But we’ve never stopped fighting -- there’s too much on the line. We are at an inflection point in the history of our nation. The American Dream and our American democracy are under attack like never before.
We are called to answer a fundamental question: who are we as Americans? It’s up to us to answer that question -- to the world, and to each other. We know we are better than this.
We’re going to need to fight to make it true. Together, we will fight to guarantee that working and middle-class families get a hand up with the largest working and middle-class tax cut in decades. Together, we will fight to declare education a fundamental right, and to guarantee that right with universal pre-k and debt-free college.
We will fight to ensure health care a fundamental human right, and ensure that right with Medicare for All. We will fight to protect our democracy and our institutions against all enemies, foreign and domestic. We will fight for an America where every single parent can send their children to school without fear of a mass killing spree. We will fight for an America where we treat attacks on voting rights, civil rights, women’s rights, and immigrant rights as attacks on our country itself.
**Together, we will stand up and fight for every single person who has ever felt vulnerable or voiceless -- because it is our duty to one another as Americans to protect one another and lift each other up.**

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Your story could change lives
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We can't keep holding people hostage by these misdemeanors.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a6dd/2f5ea88d/7978fffe/2cbdf202/3261066244/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/marijuana-criminalization-story?utm_source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&utm_campaign=mj-story-share-reg&utm_medium=ema") Friend, The movement to end the criminalization of marijuana started right here in Washington -- and now, this movement is spreading across the country at lightning speed. But as more states recognize the unfairness of marijuana prohibition, as more states recognize the harm and destruction that these laws caused, there are still thousands of people in Washington suffering from past convictions. These are people who continue to face the consequences from marijuana misdemeanors from up to 20 years ago, impeding their ability to live their day-to-day lives whether it's getting a job or buying a house. That's why Jay is committed to pardoning Washingtonians with marijuana misdemeanors this year -- and that's why we want to hear your story. If you have a story, or if your friend or family member does, please share it today -- to show the rest of Washington and the country how wrong it is to hold people hostage by these misdemeanors. [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a6dd/2f5ea88d/7978fffe/2cbdf202/3261066244/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/marijuana-criminalization-story?utm_source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&utm_campaign=mj-story-share-reg&utm_medium=ema") [**Tell us your story today -- and we might share your words to spread the message about why marijuana misdemeanors should be pardoned.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a6dd/2f5ea88d/7978fffe/2cbdf202/3261066244/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/marijuana-criminalization-story?utm_source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&utm_campaign=mj-story-share-reg&utm_medium=ema") Marijuana misdemeanors can damage a person's ability to get financing for a home. It can damage their ability to get financing for college. It can even hinder simple things like going on a field trip with your kids. The truth is that there are still too many more people throughout the country that are suffering from marijuana misdemeanor charges -- but sharing your story could help us change that for good. [**Share your story about the effects of marijuana criminalization today.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a6dd/2f5ea88d/7978fffe/2cbdf202/3261066244/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/marijuana-criminalization-story?utm_source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&utm_campaign=mj-story-share-reg&utm_medium=ema") Thank you, Team Inslee | [SHARE YOUR STORY »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a6dd/2f5ea88d/7978fffe/2cbdf202/3261066244/VEsD/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/marijuana-criminalization-story?utm_source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&source=ema_2019-01-28_mj-story-share-reg&utm_campaign=mj-story-share-reg&utm_medium=ema")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: The table is perfectly set
body:
On Friday, President Donald J. Trump set the table for a bipartisan compromise on immigration reform... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6edcca1d8d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • January 28, 2019
---
| | |
# **The table is perfectly set: It’s time for Democrats to join**

**On Friday,** President Donald J. Trump set the table for a bipartisan compromise on immigration reform, reopening the Government and giving Congress a three-week window to continue negotiations to fully fund American border security.

**“Over the next 21 days,** I expect that both Democrats and Republicans will operate in good faith,” the President announced from the White House Rose Garden. “If we make a fair deal, the American people will be proud of their Government for proving that we can put country before party.”

**Border security shouldn’t be controversial.** In fact, America has built 654 miles of barrier over the past 15 years. It’s time to listen to our Border Patrol agents and finish the job, securing areas of the border where criminals, drug cartels, and human traffickers continue to exploit vulnerabilities in our infrastructure.

**“We do not need 2,000 miles** of concrete wall from sea to shining sea—we never did. We never proposed that; we never wanted that,” the President said Friday. “Our proposed structures will be in pre-determined high-risk locations that have been specifically identified by the Border Patrol to stop illicit flows of people and drugs.”

**Experts agree.** “The president is right. The wall works,” said Mark Morgan, **[President Obama’s former Border Patrol Chief](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=be83c946d5&e=3ee4985eea)**.

**Need more evidence?** On Friday—the very day President Trump announced a 3-week window for negotiations—**[U.S. border agents seized](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b631c4818d&e=3ee4985eea)** two duffel bags filled with 17 long-arm rifles, shotguns, and ammunition on a ranch near the U.S.–Mexico border in Texas. It’s common for guns and money to flow across the border in exchange for illicit drugs.

**Republicans are ready** to make a deal that puts a stop to this lawlessness. It’s time for sensible Democrats to join them. Many have already broken ranks with House Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s comment that border walls are “immoral.” They’re right to do so: The safety and security of American citizens must always come before politics.

**[Watch President Trump’s message—“Build a wall and crime will fall!”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=51022da8a4&e=3ee4985eea)**

**More:** **[President Trump announces the end of the shutdown](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5262c7a010&e=3ee4985eea) **
